msgs.text:
Do you agree with this critique of the given proof? Is it correct? Look at the proof (attached) and the critique and explain what is going on. We have validated the candidate computationally, and most other reviewers had nothing to say. The critique: < No. There is a concrete contradiction in the load-bearing **Lemma 3.4** on page 5. The lemma asserts that the standard Demushkin relation can be obtained while also imposing the full unramified marking
[
\nu_{\rm ur}(\sigma)=1,\qquad \nu_{\rm ur}(x_0)=\nu_{\rm ur}(x_1)=0.
]


For the relation
[
x_0^2x_1^4[x_1,\sigma]=1,
]
Labute’s calculation of the unique Demushkin orientation gives, in these variables,
[
\chi(x_0)=-1,\qquad \chi(x_1)=1,\qquad
\chi(\sigma)=(1-4)^{-1}.
]
For the maximal pro-(2) Galois group of a local field, Labute identifies this orientation with the cyclotomic character, up to the harmless inverse convention. ([Cambridge University Press & Assessment][1])

But the abelianization of the displayed presentation is
[
D^{\rm ab}\cong
\frac{\mathbf Z_2\bar\sigma\oplus\mathbf Z_2\bar x_0
\oplus\mathbf Z_2\bar x_1}
{\langle 2\bar x_0+4\bar x_1\rangle}
\cong \mathbf Z_2^2\oplus C_2.
]
In particular, (\bar x_1) has infinite order. Yet Lemma 3.4 would put (x_1) simultaneously in
[
\ker(\nu_{\rm ur})\cap\ker(\chi),
]
because (\nu_{\rm ur}(x_1)=0) and (\chi(x_1)=1).

That is impossible for (G_{\mathbf Q_2}(2)). Local Kronecker–Weber and local class field theory give
[
G_{\mathbf Q_2}(2)^{\rm ab}
\cong \mathbf Z_2\times\mathbf Z_2^\times,
]
where the two coordinates are precisely the maximal unramified pro-(2) character and the (2)-adic cyclotomic character. Thus
[
(\nu_{\rm ur},\chi):
G_{\mathbf Q_2}(2)^{\rm ab}\longrightarrow
\mathbf Z_2\times\mathbf Z_2^\times
]
is injective. ([UMich Personal Web Server][2])

Consequently, the assertion in Lemma 3.4 that Labute’s normal-form corrections may all be chosen inside (\ker\nu_F) is false. The ordinary **unmarked** Demushkin presentation is valid, but it cannot have the full unramified marking claimed here.

This is fatal to the submitted proof: Proposition 3.5, the boundary fibre product in Section 4, and the terminal induction in Sections 9.1–9.3 explicitly rely on that full (\mathbf Z_2)-marking. Therefore the equality of finite-quotient counts and the final reconstruction argument are not established. The proposed abstract presentation might conceivably require a different argument, but this manuscript is not a correct proof of it.

[1]: https://www.cambridge.org/core/services/aop-cambridge-core/content/view/3045C3866BBC24C718D94C18F8F68E58/S0008414X00054158a.pdf/classification-of-demushkin-groups.pdf "https://www.cambridge.org/core/services/aop-cambridge-core/content/view/3045C3866BBC24C718D94C18F8F68E58/S0008414X00054158a.pdf/classification-of-demushkin-groups.pdf"
[2]: https://public.websites.umich.edu/~asnowden/teaching/2019/776/cft-03.pdf "https://public.websites.umich.edu/~asnowden/teaching/2019/776/cft-03.pdf"> Attached are also the research turns that generated this candidate and most of the proof - where is that going wrong, *if* the critique is right? assess.

## Assessment

**Yes. The critique is correct about the submitted proof.** Lemma 3.4 cannot be true with the displayed relation and the claimed marking. Consequently, the full-\(\mathbf Z_2\) boundary argument is not established. This does **not** by itself disprove the candidate presentation; it invalidates the proof given for it.

### The contradiction

Lemma 3.4 claims generators satisfying

\[
\nu_{\rm ur}(\sigma)=1,\qquad
\nu_{\rm ur}(x_0)=\nu_{\rm ur}(x_1)=0,
\]

together with

\[
x_0^2x_1^4[x_1,\sigma]=1.
\]

The manuscript then asserts that Labute’s normal-form changes can be made while preserving the entire map to \(\mathbf Z_2\).  

For Labute’s odd-rank \(q=2\) normal form

\[
x_1^2x_2^{2^f}[x_2,x_3],
\]

his unique orientation has

\[
\chi(x_1)=-1,\qquad
\chi(x_2)=1,\qquad
\chi(x_3)=(1-2^f)^{-1}.
\]

With \(f=2\) and the manuscript’s labels
\((x_1,x_2,x_3)=(x_0,x_1,\sigma)\), this gives

\[
\chi(x_0)=-1,\qquad
\chi(x_1)=1,\qquad
\chi(\sigma)=(1-4)^{-1}.
\]

These values are forced by the relation, not merely chosen conveniently.  Labute also identifies this canonical orientation with the local cyclotomic character, up to the usual inverse convention. 

Now abelianize the presentation:

\[
D^{\rm ab}
=
\frac{
\mathbf Z_2\bar\sigma\oplus
\mathbf Z_2\bar x_0\oplus
\mathbf Z_2\bar x_1}
{\langle 2\bar x_0+4\bar x_1\rangle}.
\]

Putting \(t=\bar x_0+2\bar x_1\), one gets

\[
D^{\rm ab}\cong
\mathbf Z_2\bar\sigma\oplus
\mathbf Z_2\bar x_1\oplus C_2t.
\]

In particular, \(\bar x_1\) is a nonzero element of infinite order.

On the other hand, local Kronecker–Weber and local class field theory give

\[
G_{\mathbf Q_2}(2)^{\rm ab}
\cong
\mathbf Z_2\times\mathbf Z_2^\times.
\]

The first coordinate is the maximal unramified pro-\(2\) character, and the second is the cyclotomic character, up to inversion and Frobenius-sign conventions. Thus

\[
(\nu_{\rm ur},\chi):
G_{\mathbf Q_2}(2)^{\rm ab}
\longrightarrow
\mathbf Z_2\times\mathbf Z_2^\times
\]

is an isomorphism, in particular injective. 

But Lemma 3.4 would give

\[
\nu_{\rm ur}(x_1)=0,\qquad \chi(x_1)=1.
\]

Therefore \(\bar x_1\) maps to the identity under an injective map, while the presentation shows that it has infinite order. That is a genuine contradiction.

There is an even sharper way to see the mismatch. In the above decomposition,

\[
\chi(t)=-1,\qquad
\chi(\sigma)=(1-4)^{-1},
\qquad \chi(x_1)=1.
\]

The first two elements supply the torsion and principal-unit coordinates of \(\mathbf Z_2^\times\). Hence

\[
\ker(\chi|_{D^{\rm ab}})=\mathbf Z_2\bar x_1.
\]

Local reciprocity therefore forces \(\nu_{\rm ur}(x_1)\) to be a \(2\)-adic unit. So the standard normal-form labeling is incompatible not only with \(\nu_{\rm ur}(x_1)=0\), but even with the claimed parity \(\nu_{\rm ur}(x_1)\equiv0\pmod2\).

### The exact false step

The unsupported sentence is:

> “Since \(r\) and \(r_0\) have the same orientation and both have zero \(\nu_F\)-image, the discrepancy at every stage lies in the image of the correction map restricted to \(\operatorname{gr}(X)\).”

Labute proves that an **unmarked** Demushkin relator can be converted to normal form by Nielsen transformations. He does not prove that these transformations can be restricted to automorphisms fixing an independently prescribed character

\[
\nu_F:F\to\mathbf Z_2.
\]

“Same orientation” and “zero total \(\nu_F\)-image” do not imply surjectivity of the correction map after restricting every correction factor to \(\ker\nu_F\). The abelianization argument above proves that such a marking-preserving automorphism cannot exist.

Conceptually, the mod-\(2\) cup form only says that \(\sigma\) and \(x_1\) form a hyperbolic pair. It does not distinguish which member is the unramified direction. The cyclotomic orientation and local reciprocity do distinguish them, and the manuscript assigns the roles incorrectly.

### Why this breaks the submitted proof

Proposition 3.5 immediately uses Lemma 3.4 to assert compatibility over the entire unramified quotient \(\mathbf Z_2\), rather than merely over some unmarked abstract quotient. Section 4 then defines

\[
\partial_{\rm bd}
=
T_{\rm tame}\times_{\mathbf Z_2}\Pi.
\]

Without Lemma 3.4, the local map to this fiber product is not justified.

The dependence is especially explicit in Section 9.1: the manuscript says that when \(H_2=C_{2^m}\), the terminal equation fixes the unramified character modulo \(2^m\), “not merely its parity,” and concludes that the two terminal exact-image problems are literally identical.  The construction of \(\alpha_2:\Pi\to H_2\) is expressly said to come from the full unramified marking of Proposition 3.5. 

So the critique is right to call the error fatal to the current argument. A minor qualification is that many later Fox–Heisenberg, duality, Fourier, or determinant lemmas may remain correct in isolation. What fails is the boundary-framed theorem into which they are assembled.

## Where the research turns went wrong

The recurring mistake was the conflation of:

\[
\text{abstract isomorphism of maximal pro-2 groups}
\]

with

\[
\text{isomorphism preserving the unramified marking}.
\]

The turns correctly found the abstract pro-\(2\) relation and its mod-\(2\) cup table. But an abstract Demushkin presentation does not remember which primitive abelian direction is arithmetic Frobenius.

Later turns tried to formalize a common elementary boundary using

\[
\partial=T\times_{C_2}\Pi_{\rm el}
\]

and explicitly described the common \(C_2\)-coordinate as “the parity of \(\sigma\).”  That is already the same hidden assumption in mod-\(2\) form: for the standard Labute labeling, the unramified character cannot vanish on \(x_1\). The final manuscript strengthened that assumed parity identification to a full \(\mathbf Z_2\)-identification by inserting Lemma 3.4. 

Thus the failure was not in the long Fourier or local-Kummer calculations. It occurred at the interface where the abstract pro-\(2\) presentation was attached to the tame quotient. The turns repeatedly audited sophisticated downstream machinery but did not perform the simpler compatibility check

\[
\text{Labute orientation}
\quad+\quad
\text{local reciprocity}
\quad+\quad
\text{abelianization}.
\]

That three-line check rules out the marking.

## Why computational validation can still succeed

There is no contradiction between:

- the candidate passing many or all prescribed finite tests; and
- Lemma 3.4 being false.

The computational verifier tests finite quotient or surjection counts of the **unmarked abstract presentation**. It does not test whether the named generator \(\sigma\) is simultaneously arithmetic Frobenius in the tame quotient and the sole unramified generator in the standard Demushkin normal form.

Consequently, the computation remains meaningful evidence for the candidate. It does not validate the relative marking used in this proof.

A repair would require a genuinely new argument: either retain the full unramified marking and use a different, non-normal-form pro-\(2\) relator containing the necessary higher terms, or rebuild the induction while explicitly tracking the actual unramified character on the standard Demushkin generators. Simply weakening “full \(\mathbf Z_2\)” to “mod \(2\)” does not automatically repair the present labeling.

The 2-adic Absolute Galois Group

Give a presentation for the absolute Galois group of the field of 2-adic numbers as a profinite group.

About the problem

Mathematician survey

About the problem

The absolute Galois group of a field  is the projective limit of all of the finite Galois groups . It packages together the information about all finite extensions, and studying the Galois group of the rational field  is a central problem in algebraic number theory. One method for approaching this group is to study the analogous Galois groups of p-adic fields . In this case, there is an explicit presentation of  for  . For  , we have a description for the absolute Galois group of some extensions of , but not for  itself. Finding such a description would fill in a gap in our explicit understanding of Galois groups, and would have consequences in terms of counting p-adic fields with a given Galois group.



Warm-up: We ask for a presentation of the absolute Galois group of , which is known.

AI prompts

Warm-up

Find a presentation for the absolute Galois group of the field of 3-adic numbers.  It may be helpful to know that the maximal pro-3 quotient of the absolute Galois group is a free group on 2 generators, and that for every finite tamely ramified extension $K/\mathbb{Q}_3$ the maximal pro-3 quotient of $\Gal(\overline{K}/K)$ is a Demuskin group.



You should give your output as two or three sections, separated by blank lines: variables, definitions (optional) and relations.



The variables section should consist of a comma separated list of variable names on one line corresponding to the generators of the presentation.  The first and second generators (say $\sigma$ and $\tau$ respectively) are distinguished, and must satisfy the implicit relation $\tau^\sigma = \tau^3$ (which should not be included in the \texttt{relations} section).  All later generators should be contained within the $3$-core of the group (the intersection of all $3$-Sylow subgroups).



In the definitions section, you may define additional variables in terms of the generators and variables defined earlier within the definitions section.  Each line should take the form "var = expr", where expr is an expression built using group operations.



In the relations section, you should give the relations as expressions in terms of variables defined in the previous two sections.  If there are multiple relations, give one on each line.



Expressions in profinite groups may involve exponents lying in $\hat{\Z}$.  To specify such a constant, you should provide its reduction modulo $5354228880 = 2^4 \cdot 3^2 \cdot 5 \cdot 7 \cdot 11 \cdot 13 \cdot 17 \cdot 19 \cdot 23$.



Example format for the solution (this is a presentation for the maximal pro-2 quotient of the 2-adic absolute Galois group



z,tau,x,y



x^2*y^3*y^z

Full problem

Find a presentation for the absolute Galois group of the field of 2-adic numbers.  It may be helpful to know that the maximal pro-2 quotient of the absolute Galois group can be presented with three generators x,y,z and one relation, x^2*y^3*y^z=1.  The theory of Demuskin groups may also be useful.



You should give your output as two or three sections, separated by blank lines: variables, definitions (optional) and relations.



The variables section should consist of a comma separated list of variable names on one line corresponding to the generators of the presentation.  The first and second generators (say $\sigma$ and $\tau$ respectively) are distinguished, and must satisfy the implicit relation $\tau^\sigma = \tau^2$ (which should not be included in the \texttt{relations} section).  All later generators should be contained within the $2$-core of the group (the intersection of all $2$-Sylow subgroups).



In the definitions section, you may define additional variables in terms of the generators and variables defined earlier within the definitions section.  Each line should take the form "var = expr", where expr is an expression built using group operations.



In the relations section, you should give the relations as expressions in terms of variables defined in the previous two sections.  If there are multiple relations, give one on each line.



Expressions in profinite groups may involve exponents lying in $\hat{\Z}$.  To specify such a constant, you should provide its reduction modulo $85667662080 = 2^8 \cdot 3^2 \cdot 5 \cdot 7 \cdot 11 \cdot 13 \cdot 17 \cdot 19 \cdot 23$.



Example format for the solution (this is a presentation for p=3)



sigma,tau,x0,x1



tau2 = tau^3011753745

sigma2 = sigma^3011753745

rho = tau2^4

angle = (x0*tau*x0^-1*tau)^2974571600

u = (x1*(rho^2)*x1*(rho^2))^2974571600

v = sigma2*tau2

w = (u*(v^2)*u^-1*(v^2))^2974571600

y1 = x1^rho*(u^v)*(w^(sigma2*tau2^2))*(w^(tau2^2))

comm = x1^-1*y1^-1*x1*y1



(x0^sigma)^-1*angle*x1^3*comm

How should we adjust the candidate and or/the proof?

According to a document from 2026-06-23, I would strengthen my earlier conclusion: **both must be fixed, but the candidate needs a targeted structural correction rather than a complete restart.**

The current \(A_2\) word should **not** be submitted unchanged. Its abstract maximal pro-\(2\) group is right, but its attachment to the tame quotient—the identification of the unramified direction—is wrong.

## Why this is a defect in the candidate itself

The candidate declares

\[
W=\langle\!\langle x_0,x_1\rangle\!\rangle
\]

to be pro-\(2\), with quotient the tame group generated by \(\sigma,\tau\). Setting \(\tau=1\), its maximal pro-\(2\) relation becomes

\[
r_{\mathrm{old}}
=x_0^2x_1^4[x_1,\sigma]=1.
\]

That is exactly the relation used throughout the manuscript. 

For this Labute normal form, the canonical Demushkin orientation is

\[
\chi(x_0)=-1,\qquad
\chi(x_1)=1,\qquad
\chi(\sigma)=(-3)^{-1},
\]

up to the inverse convention. These values are intrinsic to the presented pro-\(2\) group. Labute also identifies this orientation, for local Galois groups, with the cyclotomic character. 

But the candidate’s tame quotient intrinsically supplies

\[
\nu(\sigma)=1,\qquad \nu(x_0)=\nu(x_1)=0.
\]

Thus the infinite abelian class \(\bar x_1\) lies in

\[
\ker\nu\cap\ker\chi.
\]

For \(G_{\mathbf Q_2}(2)\), local reciprocity identifies the abelianization with the pro-\(2\) completion of

\[
\mathbf Q_2^\times
\simeq 2^{\mathbf Z}\times\mathbf Z_2^\times,
\]

where the two coordinates are unramified Frobenius and the cyclotomic/unit coordinate. Hence \((\nu,\chi)\) is injective. 

This cannot be repaired by simply “renaming generators.” The tame quotient has no nontrivial closed normal pro-\(2\) subgroup, so \(W\) is the candidate’s intrinsic maximal normal pro-\(2\) subgroup. On the local side, the corresponding subgroup is wild inertia. Any profinite isomorphism would preserve this subgroup and therefore preserve the kernel of the unramified character, up to scaling its \(\mathbf Z_2\)-coordinate. The nonzero intersection above is consequently an invariant obstruction.

So:

\[
\boxed{\text{The current candidate is not merely unproved; it has the wrong marked pro-2 shadow.}}
\]

## The marked pro-\(2\) relation that should replace it

Start with Labute’s ordinary unmarked relation

\[
a^2b^4[b,c]=1.
\]

The orientation values show that \(b\), not \(c\), is the orientation-trivial infinite direction. Local reciprocity therefore identifies \(b\) with the unramified direction. Labute’s standard relation and its orientation are given in his classification and local-field specialization. 

Set

\[
\sigma=b,\qquad x_0=ab^2,\qquad x_1=c.
\]

Then \(a=x_0\sigma^{-2}\), and

\[
a^2b^4
=(x_0\sigma^{-2})^2\sigma^4
=x_0\sigma^{-2}x_0\sigma^2
=x_0x_0^{\sigma^2}.
\]

Thus the natural fully marked relation is

\[
\boxed{
r_{\mathrm{mark}}
=x_0x_0^{\sigma^2}[\sigma,x_1]=1.
}
\]

Its abelianization is

\[
D_{\mathrm{mark}}^{\mathrm{ab}}
\simeq
\mathbf Z_2\bar\sigma
\oplus C_2\bar x_0
\oplus\mathbf Z_2\bar x_1,
\]

with, up to the orientation inverse convention,

\[
\begin{array}{c|ccc}
&\sigma&x_0&x_1\\ \hline
\nu_{\rm ur}&1&0&0\\
\chi&1&-1&(-3)^{-1}.
\end{array}
\]

This is exactly the decomposition required by

\[
\mathbf Z_2\times\mathbf Z_2^\times
\simeq
\mathbf Z_2\times C_2\times\mathbf Z_2.
\]

The manuscript should replace Lemma 3.4 by a direct proof of this marked normal form. It should not claim that arbitrary Labute corrections can be confined to \(\ker\nu\). The present Lemma 3.4 makes precisely that invalid claim. 

## A concrete repaired candidate to test

Let

\[
s=\sigma_2=\sigma^{\omega_2},
\]

and retain the current full word

\[
R_{A_2}
=x_0^2x_1^4u_1^{-1}x_1^\sigma c_0.
\]

Define

\[
r_{\mathrm{old}}(s)=x_0^2x_1^4[x_1,s],
\]

\[
r_{\mathrm{mark}}(s)
=x_0x_0^{s^2}[s,x_1],
\]

and

\[
\Delta_{\mathrm{mark}}
=r_{\mathrm{old}}(s)^{-1}r_{\mathrm{mark}}(s).
\]

The first repaired ansatz should be

\[
\boxed{
R_{\mathrm{trial}}
=
R_{A_2}\,
\Delta_{\mathrm{mark}}.
}
\]

Explicitly,

\[
R_{\mathrm{trial}}
=
x_0^2x_1^4u_1^{-1}x_1^\sigma c_0
\bigl(x_0^2x_1^4[x_1,s]\bigr)^{-1}
x_0x_0^{s^2}[s,x_1].
\]

This has three immediate advantages:

1. Modulo \(W\), the correction is trivial, so the tame quotient remains unchanged.
2. When \(\tau=1\), it reduces to \(r_{\mathrm{mark}}\), not \(r_{\mathrm{old}}\).
3. The old and corrected pro-\(2\) relators have the same quadratic Magnus initial form,

   \[
   X_0^2+\Sigma X_1+X_1\Sigma.
   \]

   Hence the mod-\(2\) Hilbert/cup table is unchanged; the correction begins only in higher filtration.

This is a **principled search seed**, not yet a proved answer. The existing \(c_0=[d_0,x_0^{\sigma_2}]\) correction may still be useful because it vanishes when \(\tau=1\), but every finite computation and every candidate-side derivative calculation must be rerun.

## What survives from the proof

A substantial amount of the architecture remains valuable:

- the marked-quotient construction;
- fixing a tame frame;
- exact-image induction;
- finite Heisenberg evaluation;
- local Tate duality and the Kummer/Hilbert calculations;
- central-cover transforms and Fourier inversion;
- profinite reconstruction from all finite quotient counts.

What does **not** survive unchanged is the candidate-side numerical input. In particular, after changing the word one must recompute:

\[
d^1_{\rho,A},
\]

the mixed Fox–Heisenberg Hessian, the coefficient pairings, the determinant quadratic form, and its Arf invariant. The existing Sections 5–8 were calculated from the old word and cannot simply be cited for the repaired one.

Proposition 3.5, the fibre product

\[
T_{\rm tame}\times_{\mathbf Z_2}\Pi,
\]

and the terminal scalar argument should then be rebuilt using

\[
\Pi_{\rm mark}
=
\left\langle
\sigma,x_0,x_1
\;\middle|\;
x_0x_0^{\sigma^2}[\sigma,x_1]=1
\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro-2}}.
\]

The current terminal argument explicitly obtains its map \(\alpha_2\) from the false full marking, so this is not a cosmetic edit. 

## Why the research turns missed it

The turns optimized two demanding but incomplete conditions:

- the \(GL_2(3)\) obstruction;
- nondegenerate tame-open cup products.

That is how the \(\sigma_2\)-commutator candidate arose. 

But they repeatedly treated

\[
\text{same abstract maximal pro-2 group}
\]

as though it implied

\[
\text{same maximal pro-2 group over the unramified }\mathbf Z_2.
\]

The distinction is invisible to:

- pure finite \(2\)-group tests;
- the ordinary unmarked Demushkin classification;
- the mod-\(2\) Hilbert table;
- many aggregate finite-quotient counts.

Indeed, \(r_{\mathrm{old}}\) and \(r_{\mathrm{mark}}\) define the same abstract Demushkin group and have the same quadratic initial form. They differ in which infinite abelian direction is attached to Frobenius. That is exactly the information the original verifier was not designed to test.

## Recommended workflow

Before rebuilding the long proof, impose three inexpensive invariant gates on every future candidate:

\[
\text{pro-2 shadow}=r_{\mathrm{mark}},
\]

\[
(\nu_{\rm ur},\chi)\text{ injective on the abelianization},
\]

\[
\text{correct mod-2 Hilbert/Bockstein table}.
\]

Then run a **marked verifier**, not only the existing unframed one: fix the tame Frobenius map and record simultaneously the unramified character modulo \(2^m\) and the canonical orientation modulo \(2^n\). Only candidates passing those tests should proceed to the \(GL_2(3)\), tame-open duality, and 5402-group sweeps.

The practical conclusion is therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Retire }A_2\text{ as written; keep its mixed correction as a component,}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{replace its pro-2 shadow by }x_0x_0^{\sigma^2}[\sigma,x_1],
\text{ and rebuild the proof around that marking.}
}
\]
